refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall support teleoperation with end-to-end communication latency that ensures safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is mostly atomic, focusing on teleoperation latency compatible with safety. The phrase 'compatible with safe remote operation' is kept to emphasize the safety aspect without specifying how latency is achieved or measured, maintaining solution neutrality. No multiple obligations are present to split.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the end-to-end communication latency during teleoperation under agreed network conditions and verify that the latency meets the safety criteria (e.g., p95 ≤ 150 ms). Conduct safety assessments to confirm that the latency does not compromise remote operation safety.

candidate_dps: Implement a communication protocol optimized for low-latency end-to-end data transfer
Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering
Incorporate adaptive latency monitoring and dynamic adjustment mechanisms
Separate teleoperation control commands from non-critical data streams to reduce latency impact
Use predictive control algorithms to compensate for communication delays
selected_dp.selected_dp: Design a system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering

potential_sources: Tight coupling between communication protocol and control logic
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on architectural design to minimize latency, which supports independence from specific communication protocols or control algorithms, reducing coupling risk.

assumptions_risks: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters to meet latency targets
Operational policies do not introduce additional delays or constraints
Real-time processing capabilities are sufficient to handle data without introducing buffering delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly defining separate DPs for communication protocol optimization and control command prioritization to further reduce coupling and improve modularity.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system architecture responsible for real-time data processing and minimal buffering strategies across the system.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Real-time data streams for processing and feedback.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication channel for data exchange with external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as NGHE Solution is designed to handle core data processing; minimal diffusion expected outside this module.
Vehicle module handles physical operations; real-time data processing is only partially contained, leading to significant diffusion.
Teleoperation involves control commands but not primary data processing; moderate containment and diffusion.
Safety Services focus on alerts and safety logic, not on data processing; poor containment and high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate boundary for containing the DP of real-time data processing with minimal buffering, as it centralizes data handling and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of data processing logic.
If real-time constraints are tightly coupled with physical vehicle operations, some processing might leak into Vehicle module.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider refining the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and physical actuation concerns, enabling clearer containment within NGHE Solution and reducing cross-module diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP logically addresses the FR by focusing on system architecture that prioritizes real-time data processing and minimal buffering, which directly supports achieving low end-to-end communication latency required for safe teleoperation.

diffusion_risks: Potential interface complexity with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules may cause some diffusion of data processing logic.
If real-time constraints are tightly coupled with physical vehicle operations, some processing might leak into Vehicle module.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion and containment score (8) for the DP, indicating good centralization of real-time data processing. However, moderate diffusion scores in Vehicle (6) and Teleoperation (5) modules suggest some risk of DP logic spreading across module boundaries, which could complicate architectural clarity and maintenance.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the IM is feasible by measuring end-to-end latency (p95 ≤ 150 ms) on the NGHE Solution module interfaces (Telemetry and Network Link) under agreed network conditions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The IM boundary 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the DP responsibilities, providing a well-defined ownership and architectural packaging that supports traceability from FR to implementation.

recommended_revisions: Refine the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and physical actuation concerns to improve containment within the NGHE Solution module and reduce diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Clarify interface definitions and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and other modules to minimize diffusion risk and improve modular cohesion.

decision.justification: Although all gates have passed indicating the current FR, DP, and IM are logically sound and feasible, the high safety criticality and noted diffusion risks suggest that further decomposition of the FR is warranted. Deriving next-level sub-FRs will help explicitly separate concerns such as data acquisition and physical actuation, improving containment and reducing diffusion across modules. This will enhance architectural clarity and support rigorous verification and maintenance.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire real-time sensor data with minimal latency
FR2: Process sensor data to generate control commands within real-time constraints
FR3: Transmit control commands reliably to physical actuators
FR4: Monitor and verify end-to-end communication latency to ensure safety requirements

revise.why: Refine the DP to explicitly separate data acquisition and physical actuation concerns to improve containment within the NGHE Solution module and reduce diffusion across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, as recommended.

assumptions: The current FR, DP, and IM are sufficiently defined to allow meaningful decomposition.
The NGHE Solution module is the primary implementation boundary for the DP.
Safety criticality requires minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment for maintainability and verification.
open_risks: Potential interface complexity causing diffusion of data processing logic across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
Tight coupling of real-time constraints with physical vehicle operations may cause processing leakage outside NGHE Solution module.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface definitions and responsibilities between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Verification data on end-to-end latency under network conditions.
Refined DP specifications separating data acquisition and actuation concerns.
